title: Helm Overview (PDX-115)
description:
Operator overview for the Helm / Symphony stack.

Wires up three data sources by name; the operator must configure them at install time:
  * `Loki`        — tails the symphony JSONL audit log (PDX-28) at
                   `~/.warp/symphony/audit.log` via promtail/vector with
                   labels {job="symphony-audit"}.
  * `Prometheus`  — scrapes the soak-harness metrics exporter (PDX-29) and
                   the symphony task counters.
  * `CF AI Gateway` — Cloudflare AI Gateway analytics, exposed via the
                   `cloudflare-mcp` Worker (PDX-76); used for tier
                   transitions per provider and MCP re-routings.

Panel -> data source mapping:
  1 tasks_dispatched_1h         -> Prometheus
  2 tasks_dispatched_24h        -> Prometheus
  3 tasks_dispatched_7d         -> Prometheus
  4 tasks_completed_failed      -> Prometheus
  5 tier_transitions_provider   -> CF AI Gateway
  6 mcp_reroutings              -> CF AI Gateway
  7 audit_severity_histogram    -> Loki
  8 soak_invariant_breaches     -> Prometheus

To import: ensure data sources named exactly `Loki`, `Prometheus`, and
`CF AI Gateway` exist in the target Grafana org, then
`Dashboards -> Import -> Upload JSON file`.

panels.title: Tier transitions per provider
panels.description: Provider-level tier upgrades / downgrades reported by the Cloudflare AI Gateway analytics MCP (PDX-76).

panels.title: MCP re-routings
panels.description: Count of MCP rerouting events (provider failover, BYOK virtual-key swap, fallback-route hit) from the AI Gateway analytics surface.

panels.title: Audit-log severity histogram
panels.description: Histogram of `action` values from the symphony audit log JSONL, grouped by severity bucket (blocked > overridden > allowed).

panels.title: Soak-harness invariant breaches
panels.description: Counter of invariant breaches emitted by the PDX-29 soak harness via `~/.warp/symphony/soak-metrics.jsonl`. A non-zero value during a soak run is an alertable condition.
